scope: spec.md + design.md + contract.md for Phase 0.5 (byte-faithful lift of mcp/ from dragoshont/homelab to dragoshont/homelab_mcp)

findings.title: Leak-scan has detection but no enforcement
findings.description: design.md §3 RK-1 mitigation listed pattern checks (hostnames, IPs, API keys, dotenv) but the 'Action' column was 'Flag/redact'. Implicit human review of the flag list was the only gate. A future operator (or a future LLM running this SDD) could miss a 'real-secret' line in leak-scan.json and commit anyway. SEA-003 risk: the gate is not asserted, only described.
findings.evidence: design.md §3 (pre-fix); contract.md MUST-PASS items did not reference leak-scan JSON.
findings.action: design.md §3 amended: the Step 5 implementation MUST refuse to run `git add mcp/` if leak-scan.json contains any entry with severity 'real-secret' or 'non-operator-copyright'. The script's exit code is the gate, not human review.

findings.title: MIT relicense assumes all source code is operator-authored
findings.description: Phase 0 PR #1 added LICENSE (MIT) to homelab_mcp covering 'all repo content'. Phase 0.5 lifts a tree authored over years that may contain vendored snippets (a function copied from StackOverflow with attribution; a regex from another OSS project; a small utility under Apache-2.0). MIT relicense is invalid for any non-operator-copyright code. Without a check, the lift will silently relicense third-party code, which is a real legal risk for a public repo.
findings.evidence: spec.md C-4 says 'operator authorizes MIT relicense for source operator's own code' but no scan validates that 'operator's own' is the universe of authors.
findings.action: design.md §3 leak-scan extended with a 'Third-party copyright' row: any `Copyright (c)`, `SPDX-License-Identifier`, or `License:` header found in lifted *.py files is flagged as 'non-operator-copyright' severity, hitting the AS-1 hard commit gate. Operator must triage every flag before Step 5 can commit.

findings.title: PowerShell Copy-Item can normalize line endings
findings.description: MP-1 requires byte-identical files (SHA-256 match). On Windows, PowerShell `Copy-Item` plus git's `core.autocrlf=true` (very common default on Windows installs) can rewrite `\n` → `\r\n` on text files between read and write, producing a destination hash that differs from the source. The check would fail at MP-1 even though the lift is logically correct, blocking the SDD on a tooling artifact rather than a real defect.
findings.evidence: design.md §2.3 (pre-fix) used `Copy-Item -LiteralPath` for all files including *.py.
findings.action: design.md §2.3 amended: use `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes` + `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllBytes` (binary copy) for *.py / Dockerfile / pyproject.toml. Hash check then operates on raw bytes both sides.

findings.title: validate_inventory only proves source repo, not lift completeness
findings.description: spec.md G-2 said `validate_inventory.py --source-repo C:\src\homelab` exit 0 proves inventory parity. But that script scans the SOURCE repo's `mcp/src/homelab_mcp/server.py`, not the LIFTED file in homelab_mcp. The check would pass even if the lift forgot a file or copied an empty file (because the source is unchanged). G-2 was therefore proving the wrong invariant.
findings.evidence: tools/validate_inventory.py reads `source_repo / 'mcp' / 'src' / 'homelab_mcp' / 'server.py'`, never the lifted path in this repo.
findings.action: design.md §2.5 added G-5 (lift-completeness): re-run the same AST scan on the LIFTED `homelab_mcp/mcp/src/homelab_mcp/server.py` and assert tool name set EQUALS the inventory. Step 5 ships a small `tools/verify_lift.py` wrapper that performs this check.

rationale: Zero critical findings. Two highs (AS-1 enforcement gap, AS-2 third-party copyright) and two mediums (AS-3 line-ending hazard, AS-4 wrong invariant) are all addressable by spec/design strengthening. All four are applied in this PR before requesting human approval at Step 5. Phase 0.5 has narrow scope (file copy) so the surface for adversarial findings was small; the four findings are honest, not padded.
